t_ch: 这个「谛听」你可能用得上，我先交给你了。
t_en: You might be able to use this Diting, so hold onto this for now.

t_ch: 没问题，就交给我吧。
t_en: No problem, leave it to me.

t_ch: 别在这里磨叽了，你快去抓人。
t_en: Enough talk. You go catch the culprit.

t_ch: 那么这里就拜托你了。
t_en: I'll leave it to you here.

t_ch: 肇事者已经跑了。
t_en: The perpetrator ran away.

t_ch: 这样吧，你留下来，我去追肇事者。
t_en: Let's split up. You stay here, I'll go after the perp.

t_ch: 我需要你找到受伤乘客后联系亲属，协助丹鼎司医者完成医疗转运。
t_en: I need you to look for injured passengers, contact their families, and help the Healers from the Alchemy Commission with the medevac.

t_ch: 需要我来做些什么？
t_en: Is there anything I can do?

t_ch: 我需要你找到受伤乘客后联系亲属，协助丹鼎司医者完成医疗转运。
t_en: I need you to look for injured passengers, contact their families, and help the Healers from the Alchemy Commission with the medevac.

t_ch: 然后，我现在就去追肇事者。
t_en: And now, I've got a perp to chase.

t_ch: 星槎良好的安全避险设计令人称赞，被撞星槎甚至看不出痕迹。
t_en: The excellent safety features of the starskiff is commendable, and there aren't even any marks on the starskiff that was hit.

t_ch: 比起星槎，你现在更应该关注受伤的乘客。
t_en: You should be more concerned about the injured passengers than the starskiff right now.

t_ch: 天舶司就在旁边…
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission is just right over there...

t_ch: 怎么敢在这里肇事？
t_en: Who would dare to stir up trouble here?

t_ch: 伤得严重不严重啊？
t_en: Are you hurt?

t_ch: 要是能帮得上忙就好了。
t_en: If only I can help in any way.

t_ch: 好像有什么纸片飞出来了…
t_en: Looks like some kind of paper flew out...

t_ch: 嘶…呼——
t_en: Sss... Ouch...

t_ch: 哎哟哟，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，俺没事……
t_en: Oh my, oh dear. {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}, I'm doing fine...

t_ch: 丹鼎司的救助马上就到了。
t_en: The Alchemy Commission's dispatch will be here soon.

t_ch: 有什么需要帮助的吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 这么明显的事故，能没事儿吗？
t_en: No way you're doing fine in a serious traffic incident like this.

t_ch: 俺…俺真的…没事——嘶，坏了，我这腰好像是有些问题。
t_en: I... I'm really... fine — Ssss, dang it. I must have strained my back or something.

t_ch: 能告诉我你亲属的联系方式吗？
t_en: Do you have any family you can contact?

t_ch: 亲属？…俺没有亲属。
t_en: Family? ...I don't have any family.

t_ch: 哎呦…{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，你帮俺…找找笔记，笔记比俺重要。
t_en: Oww... {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}, could you help look for my notes? That's worth more than my life.

t_ch: 我问的是联系方式。
t_en: I'm asking for a contact.

t_ch: 俺…带的笔记很重要，刚才掉…出去了。
t_en: My notes... are really important. They fell out.

t_ch: 笔记可以一会儿再找。
t_en: We can look for them in a bit.

t_ch: 不行，要是笔记丢了，俺就没法干下去了。
t_en: No. I'm gonna lose my job if I lose these notes.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}…求求你咧…俺的笔记很重要。
t_en: Come on, {F#young lass}{M#young fella}... Help me out... My notes are really important.

t_ch: 交给我吧，我在这附近找找。
t_en: I'll handle it. I'll look nearby.

t_ch: 笔记上有联系方式吗？
t_en: Do you have any contacts on your notes?

t_ch: …你没有通讯设备吗？
t_en: ...Don't you have a communication device?

t_ch: 没有……
t_en: No...

t_ch: 哎哟，梅姐求求你了，俺记不住事儿，全靠笔记了。
t_en: Oh, please help old Mei here. I can't remember things anymore. My notes are all I have.

t_ch: 哎哟哟，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，俺没事，稍微躺一下就能起来了。
t_en: Oh, {F#young lass}{M#young fella}, I'm fine. Just hafta lay down for a spell.

t_ch: 梅姐求求你了，俺记不住事儿，帮俺找找笔记吧。
t_en: Please help old Mei here. I can't remember things anymore. My notes are all I have.

t_ch: 散页上密密麻麻地写着家政相关的笔记，非常详细，甚至对浇不同花用多少水都有记录。
t_en: Detailed notes about housekeeping fill the scattered pages in tiny font. It even has specifications about how much water to use for each type of flower.

t_ch: 笔记上似乎只有一半的联系方式。
t_en: It seems that only half of a contact number is written in the notes.

t_ch: 散页上密密麻麻地写着家庭地址、所属登云群阁、工作时间等详细信息。
t_en: Detailed notes containing an address, Skyward Pavilions neighborhood affiliation, working hours, and more are densely packed in the scattered pages.

t_ch: 笔记上似乎只有一半的联系方式。
t_en: It seems that only half of a contact number is written in the notes.

t_ch: 散页上密密麻麻地写着食品市场的开放结束时间、不同菜品的价格、不同食品忌口等等。
t_en: Detailed notes about the business hours of grocery stores, the prices of different grocery items, dietary restrictions, and more are densely packed in the scattered pages.

t_ch: 笔记上还写着「联系方式」。
t_en: A contact number is listed in the notes.

t_ch: 尝试着联系。
t_en: Try to contact this number.

t_ch: 散页上密密麻麻地写着「广云袖」的营业时间、不同服装搭配的方式等等。
t_en: Detailed notes about the business hours of Cloudbreadth Sleeves, mix-and-match of different clothing styles, and more are densely packed in the scattered pages.

t_ch: 笔记上还写着「联系方式」。
t_en: A contact number is listed in the notes.

t_ch: 尝试着联系。
t_en: Try to contact this number.

t_ch: 两页笔记拼在一起，你得到了一个联系方式。
t_en: Combining the two pages, you obtain a contact number.

t_ch: 尝试着联系。
t_en: Try to contact this number.

t_ch: 过了一段时间，丹鼎司的医者救援队已经将梅姐带走了。
t_en: Some time has passed, and the Alchemy Commission's healers had taken Mei away.

t_ch: 之后有机会的话，再将梅姐的笔记还给她吧……
t_en: You should return Mei's notes to her when there's a chance...

t_ch: 您就是联系我的{NICKNAME}吗，请问梅姐是不是已经离开星槎海了？
t_en: Are you {NICKNAME}? The person who contacted me? Has Mei already left Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 丹鼎司医者已经把她带走了。
t_en: The healers had already taken her away.

t_ch: 没事没事，治疗最要紧，不应该等我，我之后再去丹鼎司一趟吧。
t_en: No worries. My schedule shouldn't hold up her treatment. I will visit the Alchemy Commission later.

t_ch: 你应该直接去丹鼎司医馆。
t_en: You should head to the Alchemy Commission apothecary straight away.

t_ch: 没事没事，让梅姐先去治疗吧，我也得和您见上一面。
t_en: No rush. Mei should get treated first, and I should meet you in person.

t_ch: 我临时买了一些谢礼，是仙舟甜点，还请您收下。抱歉让您费心了，为了素不相识的人，做了这么多的事情。
t_en: I bought some Xianzhou snacks on the way here as a small thank-you present. Please, take them. Thank you so much for all you've done for a complete stranger.

t_ch: 所以，麻烦您给我讲下大致的情况可以吗？
t_en: So... can you tell me what happened here?

t_ch: 吃着芦慧送来的仙舟甜点，你详细叙述了你了解的事故经过……
t_en: While enjoying the Xianzhou snacks Luhui brought, you recount the incident in detail...

t_ch: …原来是这样啊。她既不用玉兆设备，也无亲无故的，联系我算是联系对人了，要不是您好心相助，我肯定什么都不知道。
t_en: ...I see. She doesn't use the jade abacus and has no relatives, so I'm the right person for you to contact. If it weren't for you, I wouldn't have known about this.

t_ch: 梅姐这人没有家人，很久以前就在一直做家政工作，目前是我在雇佣她。
t_en: Mei doesn't have a family. She has been working as a housekeeper for a long time, and I'm her current employer.

t_ch: 她这人智力上有些缺陷，脑袋不太好，粗活可以，记事都不太行，买菜也总是出错。
t_en: Mei might not be the brightest and may even be slightly dense in the head. She's good at manual labor, but memory is not her forte. She keeps getting the wrong groceries.

t_ch: 梅姐这人确实挺笨的。
t_en: Mei does seem a little dense.

t_ch: 有这么明显吗？我和她朝夕相处的，感觉更明显一些。
t_en: Is it that obvious? For me, having her around day and night does make me more aware of her struggles.

t_ch: 不太像智力有问题呀。
t_en: She doesn't seem like a mentally challenged person.

t_ch: 三两句应该没有办法感受到，她和我朝夕相处，感受很明显。
t_en: You probably won't feel it from a quick chat. Having her around day and night does make me more aware of her struggles.

t_ch: 怎么开始说梅姐坏话了？
t_en: Why are you bad-mouthing Mei?

t_ch: 哎，我这也不是在说她坏话，我是在担心她。
t_en: I'm not bad-mouthing her. I'm just concerned for her.

t_ch: 她有时连最基础的口齿清楚都做不到，一句话通常要重复好几遍才能给她说明白。被星槎追尾了，她自己一个人糊里糊涂地肯定搞不定赔偿的事情。
t_en: Sometimes she can't even communicate on a basic level, and I would have to repeat the same sentence a few times before she understood it. Being the way she is, she won't be able to handle the compensation on her own after getting into an accident like this.

t_ch: 我得帮她把赔偿的事儿谈清楚，要是她自己来处理，肯定不了了之了。
t_en: I'll need to settle the compensation for her. If this were left to her alone, it would just fizzle out in the end.

t_ch: 您对自家保姆也太好了。
t_en: You treat your housekeeper so well.

t_ch: 你们真的不是一家人吗？
t_en: Are you really not family?

t_ch: 这个时候是不是有什么转折？
t_en: Should I anticipate a plot twist right about now?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我原本是打算今天将她辞退的。我已经想了很久了，要不是出了这个事故……
t_en: To be honest, I originally wanted to dismiss her today. It had been on my mind for a long while. If it weren't for this accident...

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: …可不能落井下石啊。
t_en: ...You can't kick her when she's down.

t_ch: 对啊，现在实在不是一个合适的时机。
t_en: That's right. This is not the right time to do so.

t_ch: 现在就是个好机会。
t_en: This is a good chance to do so.

t_ch: 您就别拿我说笑了。
t_en: Please don't tease me.

t_ch: 其实我当初雇佣她的时候也不是很满意。一来，我是看中了她要的价格低廉；二来，我了解了她的经历后，就当作自己行善扶贫了。
t_en: Actually, I wasn't super satisfied when I hired her. I only hired her because her asking price was low, for one. After learning about her experience, I also thought I'd give her a try as an act of charity.

t_ch: 记事、耳背之类的事情放在一边。她这个人思维简单，根本分不清楚什么是好赖话。
t_en: Her memory and hearing impairment aside, she's rather simple-minded and has difficulty distinguishing between praises and admonishments.

t_ch: 有时候我特别生气，声色俱厉地纠正她，她总是笑嘻嘻地让我的话从她另一只耳朵里溜出来，很快她又重蹈覆辙，我又更加生气…如此折腾，我也受不了了。
t_en: Sometimes I get so upset and scold her, but she always laughs it off. The words just go into one ear and out of the other for her, and she eventually repeats the same mistakes, which makes me even more upset... I can't stand this vicious cycle anymore.

t_ch: 那之前为什么没有辞退她呢？
t_en: So why didn't you lay her off earlier?

t_ch: 这还不至于要辞退她吧。
t_en: It's not that bad. No need to fire her.

t_ch: 我也只是一个普通人，不过是想要更好的家政服务罢了。别人都可以换，我也想雇一位交流起来更加轻松的保姆，这也不难理解吧……
t_en: I'm only an average citizen who wants better housekeeping service. I just want to get a new housekeeper like everyone else, someone I can have an easier time communicating with. That's not asking too much, is it?

t_ch: 这些年有好多次机会想要换掉，有时候我舍不得这便宜的价格，有时候也只是嫌麻烦。
t_en: I have tried so many times over the years to change her out for a different housekeeper, but I just couldn't let go of her affordability, and sometimes I feel it's just too much of a hassle to make the change.

t_ch: 更多的时候，我觉得梅姐这样的人，到哪肯定都没人要，我要是再赶走她，她无依无靠的，说不定生存下去都很困难。
t_en: But most of the time, I feel someone like Mei would not be hired anywhere else. If I dismissed her, she would have no one to turn to. She might even struggle just to stay alive.

t_ch: 我想帮梅姐把肇事赔偿处理完，再和她好好谈一谈，真希望那个时候，我还有今天的决心。对了，您见多识广，有没有什么好的建议呢？
t_en: I want to handle Mei's compensation for her before having a talk with her. Hopefully, by that point, I'll still have the same resolve as I do today. Oh, right. You seem like someone who's knowledgeable about many things. Do you have any good suggestions on how to handle this?

t_ch: 您还是应该辞退她。
t_en: I think you should fire her.

t_ch: 嗯，我会考虑考虑的，毕竟雇佣关系说白了就是等价交换，和感情无关。
t_en: Hmm, I will take that into consideration. After all, the employment relationship is an exchange of equivalents, and feelings have nothing to do with it.

t_ch: 我建议你先不要辞退她。
t_en: I would suggest that you don't fire her.

t_ch: 是这样吗？我会考虑您的建议，让我再纠结纠结吧。
t_en: Is that so? I will take that into consideration. I guess I will stay in this dilemma for a while longer.

t_ch: 我最不擅长给别人建议了。
t_en: I'm really bad at giving advice.

t_ch: 没事没事，我已经这么麻烦你了，这件事让我自己再纠结纠结吧。
t_en: No worries, I've already troubled you so much. I should sort this out on my own.

t_ch: 我就不打扰您了，今天已经耽误了不少时间，现在得去丹鼎司医馆一趟。我们之后再联系吧，辛苦您了。
t_en: Don't let me take any more of your time. I need to head toward the Alchemy Commission apothecary. Let's catch up later. Thank you for your effort.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，现在就发消息去问问芦慧吧。
t_en: I better not delay this. I'll send a text to Luhui right now.

t_ch: 你来了，我在现场找了些目击者，但是感觉证据还不全。你联系上那个…梅姐了吗？
t_en: You're here. I found some witnesses at the scene, but it seems like the evidence is not complete. Did you manage to contact... Mei?

t_ch: 梅姐又回到事故现场了。
t_en: Mei has returned to the scene.

t_ch: 她是什么铁人？这不是才出了事故吗……
t_en: Is she made of iron? She was just in an accident!

t_ch: 总之，我们先抓紧问问事故线索，再安排人手亲自把她带回医馆吧。
t_en: Anyhow, we should hurry up and ask for clues about the incident, then arrange for someone to send her back to the apothecary.

t_ch: 没有能联系上。
t_en: I failed to make the contact.

t_ch: 是吗，看起来是我的运气不太好。
t_en: Really? I guess I ran out of luck.

t_ch: 但梅姐又偷溜回到事故现场了。
t_en: But Mei apparently snuck back to the scene.

t_ch: …你能一句话说全吗？
t_en: ...Can you finish your sentence in one go?

t_ch: 我明白了，我们先抓紧问问事故线索，再安排人手亲自把她带回医馆吧。
t_en: Got it. We should hurry up and ask for clues about the incident, then arrange for someone to send her back to the apothecary.

t_ch: 我们先去找梅姐问问，再找找有没有目击者的证词吧。
t_en: Let's ask Mei first before we look for other witnesses for their accounts.

t_ch: 您好，我是刚才找过您的天舶司测速员檀檀，我想问问在刚才的事故的情况。
t_en: Hello, I'm Tantan, Speed Tester from the Sky-Faring Commission. I contacted you earlier. I would like to ask you about the traffic incident.

t_ch: 我就听到「咚——」的一声，我就跑出来了，看到人摔在地上…然后我就往星槎的航道里看，我什么都没看清，它已经跑远了。
t_en: Well, I heard a "thud" and ran outside. Seeing that person on the ground, I looked over toward the starskiff lane, but I didn't see anything. The starskiff had already escaped.

t_ch: 您再仔细回想下发生了什么。
t_en: Please try your best to recall what happened.

t_ch: 我当时离得比较远，只听到一些声音「咚——」、「哐——」、「咔嚓咔嚓——」、「咣——」。
t_en: I was quite a distance away at the time, I only heard noises like "thud," "crash," "whoosh," and "clang!"

t_ch: 音效很丰富，到底发生了什么？
t_en: Very descriptive sound effects. So, what happened?

t_ch: 我想想，我先是听到奇怪的引擎声，然后撞上了，我跑了出来，它消失不见了…重点还是那个引擎。
t_en: Let me think. First, I heard some weird engine noises, and then the collision. I ran outside, but it has already disappeared... The most important part is still the engine.

t_ch: 我听见那个<color=#dbc291ff>肇事星槎的引擎声音非常大，跟一般星槎不太一样。</color>
t_en: I heard the <color=#dbc291ff>engine sound of the offending starskiff. It was loud and different from ordinary starskiffs.</color>

t_ch: 我印象里好像没有听到这么多…
t_en: I don't remember hearing that much.

t_ch: 我这个人天生听力就很好，刚才那些也不是我胡编的。
t_en: My hearing has always been great. I didn't make those up.

t_ch: 我听见那个<color=#dbc291ff>肇事星槎的引擎声音非常大，跟一般星槎不太一样。</color>
t_en: I heard the <color=#dbc291ff>engine sound of the offending starskiff. It was loud and different from ordinary starskiffs.</color>

t_ch: 这不是相当于什么都没说吗？
t_en: That doesn't explain anything, does it?

t_ch: 你这是什么态度嘛，本来就是一个超速星槎，人家也不会停下来等我看清楚了再走啊。
t_en: What kind of attitude is that? It was a speeding starskiff. It's not like it'd slow down and wait for me to see it before driving away.

t_ch: 啊，我还想到了一点有用的信息，我这个人天生听力很好…「咚——」、「哐——」、「咔嚓咔嚓——」、「咣——」。
t_en: Oh, I recalled some information that may be useful. My hearing had always been good... I heard something like "thud," "crash," "whoosh," and "clang!"

t_ch: 我听见那个<color=#dbc291ff>肇事星槎的引擎声音非常大，跟一般星槎不太一样。</color>
t_en: I heard the <color=#dbc291ff>engine sound of the offending starskiff. It was loud and different from ordinary starskiffs.</color>

t_ch: 感谢您配合我们的调查。
t_en: Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 刚才你不是来问过一遍了吗？我再讲一次哦。
t_en: Didn't you ask me already? I'll say it one more time.

t_ch: 我听见那个<color=#dbc291ff>肇事星槎的引擎声音非常大，跟一般星槎不太一样。</color>
t_en: I heard the <color=#dbc291ff>engine sound of the offending starskiff. It was loud and different from ordinary starskiffs.</color>

t_ch: 您好，我是刚才找过您的天舶司测速员檀檀，我想问问在刚才的事故的情况。
t_en: Hello, I'm Tantan, Speed Tester from the Sky-Faring Commission. I contacted you earlier. I would like to ask you about the traffic incident.

t_ch: 哦！我记得你，我当时跑出去追那辆超速的星槎了，你就在我的背后。
t_en: Oh, I remember you! When I ran after the speeding starskiff, you were behind me.

t_ch: 当时事故刚发生，我立马就一个百米冲刺加速跑，蹭地一下冲出去了，非得跟这个肇事星槎比比高下！实不相瞒，我这个人从小就爱凑热闹。
t_en: When the incident happened, I immediately sprinted like I'd never before. I just had to race the starskiff that knocked someone down! Honestly, I just like the commotion. Has been since I was young.

t_ch: 看到什么有用的信息了吗？
t_en: Did you see anything that might help the case?

t_ch: 啊！你说到这个，我就、我就、我就！
t_en: Oh! Speaking of which, I— I— I!

t_ch: 我没看到什么，只能说结论是「星槎确实比人跑得快」。
t_en: I didn't really see anything. My takeaway was that starskiffs are faster than humans.

t_ch: 星槎的牌照看到了吗？
t_en: Did you see the starskiff registration plate?

t_ch: 啊！星槎牌照，没错、没错、没错！我刻意去记了一下！
t_en: Right! The starskiff registration plate, that's right, that's right! I memorized it!

t_ch: 是什么来着，二五六、二五七、二八二九三十一……
我忘记了！
t_en: I forgot!

t_ch: 比赛赢了吗？
t_en: Did you win the race?

t_ch: 抱歉，是我输了。有机会的话，我还要和它再比一场！
t_en: Apologies, I lost. If there is a chance, I would very much like to have a rematch!

t_ch: 我记得我追到一个十字航道之后，就什么都看不到了，你看到它去哪儿了吗？
t_en: I remember that it disappeared after I pursued it to a crossroad. Did you happen to see which way it went?

t_ch: 啊！十字航道！没错、没错、没错！你提醒了我。
t_en: Yes! The crossroad! Uh-huh, uh-huh, you reminded me.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>它朝右边拐过去了！</color>我记得很清楚。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>It made a right turn there!</color> I remember that very clearly.

t_ch: 迴星港的方向吗…感谢您配合我们的调查。
t_en: The direction of Stargazer Navalia, huh... Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 别的东西我都忘记了，就记得一件事，那艘星槎到了十字航道之后，<color=#dbc291ff>朝右边拐过去了！</color>我记得很清楚。
t_en: I do remember this, though. When the starskiff arrived at the crossroad, <color=#dbc291ff>it made a right turn there!</color> I remember that very clearly.

t_ch: 天舶司的报告说，路口的机巧鸟抓拍到了一些东西，你将梅姐处置好，一会儿也到天舶司来一趟吧。
t_en: The report from the Sky-Faring Commission says that the cycrane captured something. After you've settled Mei, come over to the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 是你…俺还记得你，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}你有看到俺的笔记吗？
t_en: It's you... I remember you. {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}, did you find my notes?

t_ch: 我找到了一些。
t_en: I found some of them.

t_ch: 原本是想还给你的。
t_en: I wanted to give it back to you.

t_ch: 捡到的所有笔记。
t_en: I picked up all of them.

t_ch: 谢谢你，就是这个，可不能随便丢了。对了，芦慧姑娘你见到了吗，哎…俺又给芦慧姑娘添麻烦了。
t_en: Thank you, there they are. I can't lose them. Oh, right, did you happen to see Ms. Luhui? *sigh* I made trouble for Ms. Luhui again.

t_ch: 见到了。
t_en: I saw her.

t_ch: 俺实在是不想麻烦芦慧姑娘，你都不知道她有多忙，有时候饭都吃不上就得离开。
t_en: I really don't want to trouble Ms. Luhui. You don't know just how busy she is. Sometimes, she had to leave for work without having a proper meal.

t_ch: 芦慧一直在担心你。
t_en: Luhui is worried about you.

t_ch: 是吗？俺身体可好了，已经出院了，她不用担心俺。
t_en: Is that so? I'm good. I even got discharged. She doesn't need to worry about me.

t_ch: 哎呀，可不能让芦慧姑娘担心俺呀，她还有好多重要的事需要担心……
t_en: Oh, I can't let Ms. Luhui worry about me. She has so many important things to worry about...

t_ch: 不好意思，请让我插一下话。我是天舶司测速员檀檀，我想问问在刚才事故的情况……
t_en: Sorry to interrupt. I am Tantan, Speed Tester from the Sky-Faring Commission. I would like to ask about the incident...

t_ch: 天舶司？这件事还要惊动天舶司吗？没有事故，俺身体没事儿，没有事故！
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission? Does this need to involve the Sky-Faring Commission? Nothing happened! I feel fine! There's no incident!

t_ch: 梅姐被摔糊涂了吗？
t_en: Did you get hit on the head too hard, Mei?

t_ch: 梅姐的头没有摔着，没事没事。你看我身体倍儿棒，不要赔偿，不用给我赔偿。
t_en: My head is fine. I'm fine, completely fine. Look, I'm doing swimmingly. I don't want compensation. I don't need it.

t_ch: 必须要找到肇事者给你赔偿。
t_en: We should find the perpetrator to compensate you for your injury.

t_ch: 我不要赔偿，不用给我赔偿。嘿嘿嘿，没事没事。
t_en: I don't want it. There's no need. Hehe, I'm alright, mighty fine.

t_ch: 咱们就当没有事故发生过吧。
t_en: Then let's pretend this never happened.

t_ch: 嘶，你怎么也这么说？
t_en: Tsk, why would you echo her?

t_ch: 小兄弟，你就听{F#她}{M#他}的吧，我没事儿，嘿嘿嘿，不需要赔偿。
t_en: Little buddy, listen to {F#her}{M#him}. I'm doing fine, hehe. I don't need any compensation.

t_ch: …这位女士，我是认真地向您询问事故的情况。
t_en: ...Madam, I'm seriously trying to ask about the traffic incident.

t_ch: 天舶司的老爷也行行好，俺不想再给芦慧姑娘添麻烦了，咱们就当没有事故发生过吧。
t_en: Please, Sky-Faring Commission boss, have mercy! I don't want to cause any more trouble for Ms. Luhui. Let's pretend this never happened.

t_ch: 放心，我们不会索要赔偿的。
t_en: Don't worry. We won't ask for compensation for you.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}谢谢你，这样就好。
t_en: Thank you, {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}. This is fine as it is.

t_ch: 这位女士，请您配合。
t_en: Madam, please cooperate.

t_ch: 请您配合天舶司工作。
t_en: Please cooperate with the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}…好吧，听你的。
t_en: {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}... Okay, I will do what you want me to do.

t_ch: …我看你们有话要说，等会儿吧。现在我们先完成事故调查。
t_en: ...I see you have some things to discuss. Please hold that thought. Let's complete the investigation first.

t_ch: 看清楚肇事星槎了吗？
t_en: Did you manage to see the offending starskiff?

t_ch: 俺两眼一黑，没有看清楚，就摔出去了。好像是艘<color=#dbc291ff>蓝色</color>的星槎，又好像是<color=#dbc291ff>黄色</color>的……
t_en: I blacked out. I didn't see anything when I got thrown outside. It could be a <color=#dbc291ff>blue</color> starskiff, but it could also be <color=#dbc291ff>yellow</color>...

t_ch: 事故是怎么发生的呢？
t_en: How did the collision happen?

t_ch: 当时俺刚搭上星槎，还没有来得及系好安全带，就感觉背后发出一声巨响，随后，俺就飞出去了。这就是所有过程了……
t_en: I was just boarding a starskiff. I didn't even get to tie my safety belt when I heard a loud bang from behind. Then I flew out. This is all I know...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>当时俺听到了「咔嚓」一声</color>…是不是星槎被撞坏掉了，俺不用赔偿吧？
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I remember hearing a cracking sound</color>... Is the starskiff damaged? I don't need to pay for that, do I?

t_ch: 还记得什么吗？
t_en: Do you remember anything else?

t_ch: 俺就记得今天油鸡店有折扣，芦慧姑娘从前就爱吃这些不健康的东西…她也挺长时间没吃了，所以俺就想买一点给她。
t_en: I remember a discount at the roasted chicken store today. Ms. Luhui had always liked these unhealthy foods... It's been a while since she had any, so I wanted to buy some for her.

t_ch: 别的什么俺都不记得了。
t_en: I can't remember anything else.

t_ch: 梅姐虽然说了却又像是没说，我们再去问问其他的目击证人吧。
t_en: There's nothing we can use from Mei's statement. Let's go and ask other witnesses.

t_ch: 抱歉，俺没有帮上什么忙。
t_en: Sorry I couldn't help much.

t_ch: 拿到这些笔记之后，俺就该回去干活了，谢谢你了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。
t_en: Once I get the notes, I need to head back and continue work. Thank you, {F#young lass}{M#young fella}.

t_ch: 别着急干活，先养伤吧。
t_en: Don't rush back into work. Get some rest first.

t_ch: 诶，那可不行。芦慧姑娘要是没有我照顾，就没人能照顾她了。
t_en: Oh, that won't do. If I don't take care of Ms. Luhui, no one could.

t_ch: 你别看她这个人和声细语的，但是她能调度好几百号人的拍摄现场，可有本事了。
t_en: She may be gentle and soft-spoken, but she can command a film set with hundreds of people working. She's so capable.

t_ch: 芦慧知道了你这样会生气的。
t_en: Luhui will get upset to see you rushing back to work like this.

t_ch: 芦慧姑娘生气了不打紧，她总是很生气，只要对她和和气气的，不顶嘴，她就会自然消气了，俺已经习惯了。
t_en: It's okay if she's upset. She's always upset. But if you act nice with her and don't talk back at her, she will naturally cool down. I'm used to it.

t_ch: 她才没有空操心俺呢，她是大制作人，能调度好几百号人的拍摄现场，可有本事了。
t_en: She has no time to worry about me. She's a huge producer who commands a film set with hundreds of people working. She's so capable.

t_ch: 不说了啊，梅姐先去医馆一趟，不让人操心。{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}你也忙去吧。
t_en: I should stop chattering about and go to the apothecary so other people won't worry about me. {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}, go ahead and get busy.

t_ch: 梅姐，有件事我想告诉你…
t_en: Mei, there's something I want to tell you...

t_ch: 还、还有什么事情吗？
t_en: I—is something else the matter?

t_ch: 芦慧姑娘想要解雇您。
t_en: Ms. Luhui wants to fire you.

t_ch: …是真的吗？俺不信。
t_en: ...Is that true? I don't believe you.

t_ch: 要是真的也不打紧，俺先去把现在要做的事情做了。
t_en: Even if it's true, it doesn't matter. I need to go do what I need to do.

t_ch: …没事儿，我忘了想说什么了。
t_en: ...It's nothing. I forgot what I wanted to say.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，你可把俺吓坏了。俺还以为芦慧姑娘说了什么「梅姐摔伤了，该解雇了」之类的话。
t_en: Hahaha, you scared me half to death. I thought Ms. Luhui might say something like "Mei is hurt, so it's time to fire her" or something.

t_ch: 俺没事儿，俺还能干，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}要是没什么想说的话，再见。
t_en: I'm okay. I can still work. If there's nothing else, {F#Young lass}{M#Young fella}, I bid you goodbye.

t_ch: 梅姐再见。
t_en: Goodbye, Mei.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}再见。
t_en: Goodbye, {F#young lass}{M#young fella}.

t_ch: 听说这里可以调查事故影像…
t_en: Is this where we can check on the accident footage?

t_ch: …事情我都听檀檀说过了，没想到你才做了半天的测速员，就遭遇交通事故了。
t_en: I've heard all about it from Tantan. I didn't expect you to have a traffic accident after only half a day as a speed tester.

t_ch: 每次都能在事故发生的时候出现，这究竟又是一种怎样的才能呢？
t_en: Isn't it a talent of some kind to be able to show up every time an accident happens? Wouldn't you say so?

t_ch: 夕葵姐姐说笑了。
t_en: I like your sense of humor.

t_ch: 好了好了，是才能的话，可得好好利用才行。
t_en: Well, if it's a talent, it must be put to good use.

t_ch: 没想到夕葵姐姐性格如此恶劣…
t_en: Didn't think you'd be so witty...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我只是阐述了一个事实而已。
t_en: Haha, I'm just stating a fact.

t_ch: 敢在司辰宮外超速，区区肇事飞行士胆子不小，也太不把天舶司放在眼里了。
t_en: This pilot dares to speed at a place just outside the Palace of Astrum. They have no respect for the Sky-Faring Commission!

t_ch: 你和檀檀在现场调查取证了不少，比对现场的机巧鸟影像，应该就能找到肇事的星槎。
t_en: You and Tantan have done an investigation on the field. If we match the cycrane footage, we should be able to find the starskiff that caused the accident soon.

t_ch: 现在要先看哪一处机巧鸟的影像？
t_en: Which cycrane footage do you want to see first?

t_ch: 西南口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the southwest entrance.

t_ch: 从这个角度上来看，好像有星槎的引擎有些问题。
t_en: From this angle, it seems that there is some problem with the starskiff's engine.

t_ch: 不像是通用的部件…有可能涉及非法改造。
t_en: Doesn't look like the original engine... looks like it's been illegally modified.

t_ch: 东南口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the southeast entrance.

t_ch: 这个角度上看，不同星槎的颜色很明显。
t_en: From this angle, the colors of the different starskiffs are obvious.

t_ch: 不知道你们问到了多少目击证词。
t_en: I don't know how much eyewitness testimony you got.

t_ch: 东北口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the northeast entrance.

t_ch: 这张照片比较有用，能看到不同的星槎去往了不同方向。
t_en: This photo is more useful, you can see different starskiffs going in different directions.

t_ch: 你们有打听到肇事星槎去了哪里吗？
t_en: Have you found out where the offending starskiff went?

t_ch: 照片就交给我和檀檀吧。
t_en: Leave the photo to me and Tantan.

t_ch: 那这件事情就交给你和檀檀了。
t_en: I'll leave this to you and Tantan.

t_ch: 接下来就该找找肇事星槎了。
t_en: It's time to find the offending starskiff.

t_ch: 通过你的问询、我的调查，我们得到了一些<color=#dbc291ff>证言</color>，结合机巧鸟的影像，我们就能找到目标星槎到底是什么了。
t_en: Through your inquiry and my investigation, we got some <color=#dbc291ff>testimonies</color>. With these testimonies and the cycrane footage, we should be able to find the starskiff we're looking for.

t_ch: 想再看看照片。
t_en: I want to see the photo again.

t_ch: 机巧鸟抓拍下的影像会带有很多信息，你想看哪个？
t_en: Cycrane footage definitely provides a lot of useful information. Which one would you like to see?

t_ch: 西口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the west entrance.

t_ch: 西口的机巧鸟
t_en: Cycrane at the west entrance

t_ch: 东口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the east entrance.

t_ch: 东口的机巧鸟
t_en: Cycrane at the east entrance

t_ch: 北口的机巧鸟。
t_en: Cycrane at the north entrance.

t_ch: 北口的机巧鸟
t_en: Cycrane at the north entrance

t_ch: 照片我已经很清楚了。
t_en: I have a clear understanding of the photo.

t_ch: 想再看看证言。
t_en: I want to go over the testimonies again.

t_ch: 我把证人们的证言都记下来了，你想问谁的？
t_en: I've recorded all the eyewitness testimonies. Whose testimony do you want to check?

t_ch: 有些迷糊的女士说了什么。
t_en: The clumsy lady's testimony.

t_ch: 她听见那个<color=#dbc291ff>肇事星槎的引擎声音非常大，跟一般星槎不太一样。</color>
t_en: She heard the <color=#dbc291ff>sound of the engine of the starskiff that had caused the accident. It was loud and different from ordinary starskiffs.</color>

t_ch: 这可能是那艘星槎涉及非法改装，有些人喜欢把军用级别星槎引擎改到民用星槎上，声音非常大…总之，这句证言挺有参考价值。
t_en: This may be because that starskiff has been illegally modified. Some people like to install military-grade starskiff engines onto civilian starskiffs, resulting in a loud sound when driving... In essence, this testimony is of great value.

t_ch: 突然兴奋的男士说了什么。
t_en: The suddenly excited man's testimony.

t_ch: 他追逐那艘星槎到了十字航道之后，<color=#dbc291ff>星槎朝右边拐过去了！</color>
t_en: He chased the offending starskiff to the crossroad and saw it <color=#dbc291ff>make a right turn there!</color>

t_ch: 我们追逐着星槎从北到南，也就是说星槎去向了西方。
t_en: We chased it from north to south. Making a right turn means that it went to the west.

t_ch: 梅姐说了什么。
t_en: Mei's testimony.

t_ch: 梅姐记忆不清，我也问过她几次，她只说星槎可能是<color=#dbc291ff>蓝色</color>的，又可能是<color=#dbc291ff>黄色</color>的，也许两者都不是。
t_en: Mei's memory is not very good. I asked her several times. She said the color of that starskiff looks like <color=#dbc291ff>blue</color>, but it could also be <color=#dbc291ff>yellow</color>, or maybe neither.

t_ch: 她说自己听到了<color=#dbc291ff>当时俺听到了「咔嚓」一声</color>…但是这也说明不了什么。
t_en: Mei said she <color=#dbc291ff>heard a crack sound...</color> But this doesn't mean anything.

t_ch: 证言我已经很清楚了。
t_en: The testimonies are clear to me.

t_ch: 我已经知道真相了。
t_en: I already know what happened.

t_ch: 就以这张照片为参照吧，哪一艘才是肇事星槎呢？
t_en: Which is the offending starskiff in this picture?

t_ch: 从左数第一艘。
t_en: The first one from the left.

t_ch: 就是这艘了，我来查查它的记录。
t_en: Alright, let me check this starskiff's record.

t_ch: 从左数第二艘。
t_en: The second one from the left.

t_ch: 就是这艘了，我来查查它的记录。
t_en: Alright, let me check this starskiff's record.

t_ch: 从左数第三艘。
t_en: The third one from the left.

t_ch: 就是这艘了，我来查查它的记录。
t_en: Alright, let me check this starskiff's record.

t_ch: 从左数第四艘。
t_en: The fourth one from the left.

t_ch: 就是这艘了，我来查查它的记录。
t_en: Alright, let me check this starskiff's record.

t_ch: 通过一番调查比照，檀檀找到了肇事星槎的资料。
t_en: After investigation and comparison, Tantan manages to find data on the offending starskiff.

t_ch: 嫌疑人之前就有非法改造星槎犯罪前科，事故发生前嫌疑人从迴星港出发，事故发生后不久便返回迴星港。
t_en: The suspect has a criminal record of modifying starskiffs. The suspect departed from Stargazer Navalia before the accident occurred, then returned to Stargazer Navalia after the accident.

t_ch: 看上去像是在公共航道上进行星槎引擎测试，肇事后逃逸，现在应该还在迴星港。
t_en: It looks like the suspect was testing their starskiff engine on a public lane. After the accident, they ran away. They should be in the Stargazer Navalia by now.

t_ch: 你的推理应该没有问题，走吧，我们去迴星港抓捕犯人。
t_en: Your inference seems right. Let's go to Stargazer Navalia to catch the suspect.

t_ch: 通过一番调查比照，檀檀发现这个推理结果有些问题……
t_en: After investigation and comparison, Tantan feels there is a problem with this thinking...

t_ch: 我比对了好几次，结果好像不太对。我是说有没有一种可能是…推理本身出问题了。
t_en: I checked it several times and it didn't seem quite right. I mean, is it possible that... there is something wrong with the reasoning.

t_ch: 出什么问题了，我曾经也在天舶司处理过一段时间交通事故，我来帮你们看看吧。
t_en: What seems to be the problem? I used to solve traffic accident problems at the Sky-Faring Commission. Let me help you.

t_ch: 经过十字航道后，向西行驶…应该是蓝色或者黄色船身…应该是这艘去往迴星港的船只。
t_en: A blue or yellow starskiff turned west at the crossroad... It should be this starskiff heading toward the Stargazer Navalia.

t_ch: 能「发出了巨大的声音」，应该是指非法改造的引擎。估计它还在迴星港里，你们要是现在赶过去，应该能人赃俱获。
t_en: It made a deafening sound. Its engine must have been illegally modified. There is a great chance that this starskiff is still in Stargazer Navalia. If you go there right now, you should be able to catch it.

t_ch: 岩明大人的推理应该没有问题，走吧，我们去迴星港抓捕犯人。
t_en: Yanming's inference seems right. Let's go to Stargazer Navalia to catch the suspect.

t_ch: 能帮到你们就好。
t_en: Glad to help.

t_ch: 这就是那艘违法改造的星槎，仅从外壳上来看，和一般的星槎没有什么差别。
t_en: This is the illegally modified starskiff. From its appearance, it's no different from an ordinary starskiff.

t_ch: 船首附近的龙骨有着明显断裂，这似乎解释了「咔嚓」一声究竟由何而来。
t_en: The keel near the bow was broken, which seems to explain the crack sound.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这就是事故中嫌疑最大的星槎。
t_en: Without a doubt, this is the prime suspect of the starskiff accident.

t_ch: 哟，看什么看，看什么看，没见过竞赛级的星槎吗？
t_en: Hey, what are you looking at? You've never seen a competition-level race starskiff before, have you?

t_ch: 没见过这么破的。
t_en: Never seen a beat-up vehicle like this.

t_ch: 哈？我没有听错吧，不会是有人嫉妒我吧。
t_en: You are just jealous of me.

t_ch: 我这星槎只是小剐小蹭，不碍事的。要怪就怪航道上的人没长眼。
t_en: My starskiff is just a little scratched, no big deal. If it weren't for those blind drivers on the lane, it would still be in perfect condition!

t_ch: 这是你的吗，你得意什么？
t_en: Is this really yours?

t_ch: 这就是我的星槎，哎哟喂，你不会是嫉妒我了吧，我就喜欢这样的眼神。
t_en: Of course it's mine. You're not jealous, are you?

t_ch: 你要是想在它面前拍张照也是可以的。
t_en: You can take a photo with it if you want.

t_ch: 这简直是大师杰作！
t_en: This is truly a masterpiece!

t_ch: 没想到你还有点儿眼力价嘛，你要是想在它面前拍张照也是可以的。
t_en: You do have some taste. If you want to take a photo with my starskiff, that's fine.

t_ch: 您好，我是天舶司管理航道安全的执法人员檀檀，这是我行政执法的证件。
t_en: Hello, I am Tantan, a law enforcement officer of the Sky-Faring Commission who manages lane safety. Here's my ID.

t_ch: 因为您可能涉及违规驾驶、非法星槎改造等情况，我们将依法扣押这艘星槎，登记并妥善保管，调查完毕后及时发还…
t_en: You are suspected of being involved in a traffic violation and illegal starskiff modifications. By law, we will confiscate this starskiff and return it after the investigation ends.

t_ch: 你以为你口说无凭讲了半天，我就信吗，这是法治社会，证据呢？
t_en: I'll believe you when I see the evidence. Where is the rule of law? Do you have any evidence to prove that?

t_ch: 选择符合情景的路况照片。
t_en: Select the right photo evidence of the accident.

t_ch: …这，这确实是我的星槎。
t_en: This... is my starskiff.

t_ch: 选择不符合情景的路况照片。
t_en: Select the wrong photo evidence of the accident.

t_ch: 喂喂喂，认真点儿，把真正的证据拿给他看。
t_en: Come on. Can you take this seriously? Show him the actual evidence.

t_ch: 啧，本来只想唬一下的，没想到还真的被拍下来了。
t_en: Ugh, I was just bluffing. Didn't think you'd have this hard evidence.

t_ch: 天舶司就只有你们两个人是吧，那你们也别想抓住我了！
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission only sent you to catch me, right? I don't think you will succeed in your mission!

t_ch: 快给我拦住他们，铁疙瘩。
t_en: Stop them, bucket of bolts!

t_ch: 涉嫌袭击天舶司执法人员，我将依法刑事拘留，新账旧账你都逃不掉。
t_en: I am placing you in criminal detention for assaulting law enforcement officers of the Sky-Faring Commission. There is no way you can escape from this.

t_ch: 受害人那边…我想起来了，梅姐是没有联系方式对吗？那就麻烦你联系一下她的雇主。
t_en: As for the victim... We don't have Mei's contact information, right? Please get in touch with her employer.

t_ch: 赔偿的事怎么样了？
t_en: How about compensation?

t_ch: 你放心，虽然交通肇事我处理得不多，但是交通肇事的后续赔偿我非常熟悉。
t_en: Don't worry. Although I don't have much experience with traffic accidents, I am very familiar with compensation matters.

t_ch: 天舶司之后还会去联系梅姐的，你现在告诉他们「抓住了肇事者」就好。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission will contact Mei later. For now, you can just tell them that we have arrested the suspect.

t_ch: 还得是天舶司去联系她。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission will contact her.

t_ch: 天舶司之后还会去联系梅姐的，你现在告诉他们「抓住了肇事者」就好。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission will contact Mei later. For now, you can just tell them that we have arrested the suspect.

t_ch: 交通肇事的后续赔偿还得需要本人参与，你和那位雇主也代劳不了。
t_en: The matter of compensation must be taken care of by the victim herself. You and her employer cannot represent her.

t_ch: 这边的事情就先交给我吧。
t_en: Leave this matter to me.

t_ch: 我联系到了认识的律师，她能够帮忙梅姐处置赔偿的事情。这都是多亏了{NICKNAME}，麻烦您忙东忙西的，这是我的一点心意。
t_en: I've contacted a lawyer I know to help Mei with the compensation matter. It's all thanks to your help, {NICKNAME}. Please accept this little token of my gratitude.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}谢谢你啊。
t_en: Thank you, {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 其实俺不需要赔偿，只要还能工作，咱就感恩了。
t_en: Actually I don't need compensation. I'm grateful as long as I can still work.

t_ch: 该拿的赔偿一分都不能少。
t_en: Take every bit of compensation you deserve.

t_ch: 赔偿就是赔偿，是法律要求他给你的，你不要的话，就是违法。
t_en: If you don't accept the compensation from him, you're breaking the law.

t_ch: 好的好的，芦慧姑娘说了算，你说俺要俺就要，你说俺不能要，俺就不要。
t_en: Alright, I'll listen to you. If you say I have to take it, I will take it. If you say I can't, then I won't.

t_ch: …哎，你根本就没有把我的话听进去。
t_en: Aaaah... You still don't get it.

t_ch: 芦慧姑娘能让你丢了工作吗？
t_en: Are you worried that Ms. Luhui will fire you?

t_ch: 俺可没有这么说…俺就是担心，芦慧姑娘会不会嫌弃我手脚不灵活了。俺还能干，姑娘别担心。
t_en: I'm not saying that... I'm just worried that Ms. Luhui would hate me for being clumsy. I can still work.

t_ch: 又不是芦慧姑娘要赔偿你。
t_en: It's not Ms. Luhui who needs to compensate you.

t_ch: 都怪俺嘴笨，俺不是这个意思…芦慧姑娘莫生气。
t_en: All my stupid words... That's not what I meant... Please don't be mad, Ms. Luhui.

t_ch: 梅姐…我想这件事已经想了很久了。这次雇佣合同到期了之后，我不想再续签了，希望你可以理解。
t_en: Mei... I've been thinking about this for a long time. After your current employment contract expires, I'm afraid I won't extend it. I hope you can understand.

t_ch: 啊？那姑娘你要怎么办，再请一个人吗？
t_en: Hah? What are you going to do then? Are you going to recruit someone else?

t_ch: 啊…关于这个事嘛，你放心，我不会去请别人。
t_en: Ah, about that... Relax, I won't recruit anyone else.

t_ch: 放心？怎么放心，家里活儿很多的嘞！
t_en: Relax? How can I relax? How are you going to do all the chores by yourself?

t_ch: 芦慧姑娘自己忙得过来，对吧？
t_en: Ms. Luhui can finish them alone, right?

t_ch: …诶，没错，我自己能处理得过来。
t_en: Ahh... That's right. I'll be fine.

t_ch: 你是担心芦慧要去雇别人吗？
t_en: Are you worried that Luhui is going to hire someone else?

t_ch: 芦慧姑娘，你最好还是再请一位。
t_en: Ms. Luhui, it is best that you hire another housekeeper to help you.

t_ch: 我现在不会去请别人。
t_en: For now I'm not planning to.

t_ch: 是吗…那这样吧，俺来告诉你，家里大大小小的事情该要怎么做，这样俺才会放心。
t_en: Then... let me tell you how to do all the housework. That way I can put my mind at ease.

t_ch: 梅姐又拿出了她买菜时记菜品和账目的小本，让芦慧仔细查看……
t_en: Mei takes out a note and a vegetable shopping list and asks Luhui to take a closer look at them.

t_ch: 从这里到这里，讲的是房间清洁的顺序…啊，对了，芦慧姑娘你不爱丢垃圾，垃圾全部堆在厨房旁边，始终不记得去丢。听梅姐的，垃圾可不能过夜啊。
t_en: From here to here, it's about the order of cleaning the rooms... Oh, another thing, you always leave trash in the kitchen and forget to take them out. Now that I'm gone, you need to remember not to let the trash pile up for more than one night.

t_ch: 好…这画的几个方块是什么？
t_en: Okay... What are these squares over here?

t_ch: 洗碗布放在这个柜子里，擦桌子的布，我放在厨房第二个柜门上边，厨房用布也单独分出来了……
t_en: The dishcloth is placed in this cabinet. As for the cloth for wiping the table, I put it on the second cabinet door in the kitchen, and the kitchen clothes are placed separately...

t_ch: 你看这一页，是宝宝挑食的食物，这些东西做了他也不会吃，你切得再碎他也吃得出来。这些个是营养搭配的菜单，你爱吃辣的，但是不能一直吃，会上火……
t_en: Look at this page. It has all the names of the foodstuffs that the baby hates to eat written on it. He won't eat these, even if you cut them into pieces. These are the meals I planned that will guarantee a superb supply of nutrition. You love spicy food, but don't eat too much or you will get a heartburn...

t_ch: 好详细啊。
t_en: This is really detailed.

t_ch: 这就是梅姐要找回来的笔记…
t_en: So this is the note that Mei wants to retrieve.

t_ch: 俺头脑不聪明，只好全都记下来了。你看就算俺不在了，这本笔记也能发挥用处，嘿嘿。
t_en: I know my mind isn't that sharp, so I jotted down everything. Even if I'm gone later, these notes will still be very useful to Ms. Luhui.

t_ch: 这个「浇花」这里，好像画了一个杯子，然后这里三分之一的水是什么意思啊，是只用倒这么多的水吗？
t_en: In the "Water the Flowers" section, you drew a glass that is one-third full of water. What does this mean? Is this the amount of water you use to water the flowers each time?

t_ch: 这是每天浇花时倒的营养液，你说的每次都要倒这么多，多了不行，少了不行。
t_en: No. It's the amount of nutrient solution that must be used when watering flowers. Ms. Luhui said that every time the amount must be just right, it can't be too much or too little.

t_ch: …连我自己都忘记了。
t_en: I forgot about this myself...

t_ch: 那一页又是什么呢？「好心人，帮我找到了笔记，测速小伙子的同事，要感谢{F#她}{M#他}……」
t_en: What is this page about? "A kind young {F#lady}{M#man} helped me find the notebook. Must thank {F#her}{M#him}."

t_ch: 这写的是我呀？
t_en: That's me!

t_ch: 哎哟，芦慧姑娘，这一页俺就自己留下吧。
t_en: Ahh. Ms. Luhui, I want to keep this page to myself.

t_ch: 没事儿，我也得记着好好感谢{F#她}{M#他}呀。
t_en: I also have to thank {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 梅姐一字一句地向芦慧交代了家里各种活计的细节，你注意到芦慧眼角有泪。
t_en: Mei explains the details of the housework to Luhui, and you notice that Luhui has tears in her eyes.

t_ch: 梅姐将笔记交到了芦慧的手上，然后独自离开了这里，她还得帮芦慧接小孩回家。
t_en: Mei hands the notebook to Luhui and leaves the room to pick up Luhui's child.

t_ch: 抱歉，我刚才有些走神。
t_en: Sorry, I got lost in thought.

t_ch: 现在去追还来得及哟。
t_en: It's still not too late to chase her back.

t_ch: 您就别拿我说笑了。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 你在后悔吗？
t_en: Are you regretting it?

t_ch: 下了决心之后再后悔吗？可以这样啊。
t_en: Regret making a decision like this? Can I still...

t_ch: 做好决定了，就不要后悔。
t_en: Don't have regrets after making a decision.

t_ch: 你说的对，现在不是后悔的时候…吗？
t_en: Right, now's not the time for regrets... is it?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，我才明白，原来梅姐提心吊胆的，并不是她有多舍不得这份工作。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I just realized that Mei is not worried about losing her job.

t_ch: 她是在担心没人照顾你的生活。
t_en: She is worried that no one can take care of you after she is gone.

t_ch: 嗯，对呀，原来她是在担心我。
t_en: Yes, she is worried about me.

t_ch: 那她是在担心什么呢？
t_en: So what is she worried about?

t_ch: 她是在担心没人照顾我的生活。
t_en: She is worried that no one can take care of me after she is gone.

t_ch: 我将梅姐看作是一个干活不称职的帮手，但是在梅姐心里，我只是她辛苦拉扯竭力照顾的…家人，哎。
t_en: I've always thought of Mei as an incompetent worker. But in Mei's heart, I'm a family member she has to take care of... *sigh*

t_ch: 你听过我抱怨了很多次梅姐，但是她给我翻看她的小本子的时候，记下了很多「我说过的话」，我才知道自己也不算是一个好的雇主。
t_en: You've heard me complain about Mei many times. I didn't know that I was not a good employer until I saw her notebook. She has written down so many things that I said.

t_ch: 考虑到梅姐的智力，我对她远低于市场价格的薪资心安理得；心情差的时候，我也老是对她摆臭脸，给她立下了一个又一个的「规矩」…她也全部记下来了。
t_en: Considering Mei's slowness, I have no qualms about paying her well below the market average. When I'm in a bad mood, I always get mad at her and set a lot of "rules" for her, and she wrote them all down without complaint.

t_ch: 看着她的小本子，我才意识到，她照顾我已经有两百年了。
t_en: After looking at the notes, I realized that she has taken care of me for two hundred years.

t_ch: 两百年，对化外民来说一定很漫长吧，对我来说这也应该很漫长的，为什么我把这漫长的一切都理所当然了呢？
t_en: Two hundred years must be a long time for Outworlders, and it's also quite a long time for me. Why did I take everything for granted?

t_ch: 因为她就像是家人一样。
t_en: Because Mei is just like family to you.

t_ch: …哎，你说的没错，她就像是家人一样，我才会这样理所当然。
t_en: You're right. She's like family. That's why I took her for granted.

t_ch: 仙舟上没有老无所依的故事吗？
t_en: So Xianzhou has no popular stories about the old and the helpless?

t_ch: 为什么突然问起这个问题，仙舟上没有老人…
t_en: Why would you ask this? There are no old people on Xianzhou...

t_ch: 你是想说，仙舟人不像化外民一样会怕老，所以不会害怕自己老无所依吗？
t_en: You mean people on Xianzhou don't age like Outworlders, so they aren't afraid of getting old and ending up living on the streets?

t_ch: 真是个奇怪的角度啊，她不会老去，我也不会老去。我没有察觉到她的辛酸，连她自己也没有察觉。
t_en: What an odd but reasonable point of view. Mei won't age, and neither will I. I was not aware of Mei's hardship, nor was she aware of it.

t_ch: 这样想想，不会老去也有这样的坏处呀……
t_en: If you think about it, not being able to age has its downsides too, huh...

t_ch: 对了，我之前问过您两次，原本希望能够得到您的建议…在雇佣关系里，我到底应该将梅姐看作是一位按时计薪的家政妇，还是一位陪伴许久的家人？
t_en: By the way, I asked you twice before and was hoping for your advice. In an employer-employee relationship, should I think of Mei as an hourly paid housemaid, or as a family member who has been there with me for a long time?

t_ch: 这一次我就不问您了，我在刚才找到了自己的答案。
t_en: But this time I don't need to ask anymore. I already have the answer myself.

t_ch: 我会在这里待一会儿，就不再打扰您了，祝您的生活愉快。
t_en: I'll stay here for a while. I should stop bothering you now. Have a nice day.

t_ch: 寻找丢失的笔记，「谛听」应该能派上用场……
t_en: Looking for a missing notebook, Diting might come in handy...

t_ch: …我只是有些恍惚。
t_en: ...I'm just a bit confused.

t_ch: 您是我生活里的陌生人，我能很快察觉到您的善意，我也想积极回应您。
t_en: Even though you are a complete stranger to me, I can already feel how kind you are. And I really want to do my best to return the favor.

t_ch: 但是梅姐…我对她太过熟悉了，反而注意不到她在为我做什么。
t_en: As for Mei... I took her for granted and failed to notice all she has done for me.

t_ch: 怪我多嘴，您也会有觉得家人的关怀是理所当然的时候，可别像我一样习以为常。
t_en: Forgive me if I'm being nosy. But whenever you feel that being taken care of by a dear family member is a given, please don't take it for granted like I did.

t_ch: 我会在这里待一会儿，就不再打扰您了，祝您的生活愉快。
t_en: I'll stay here for a while. I should stop bothering you now. Have a nice day.

t_ch: 梅姐的笔记
t_en: Mei's Notes

t_ch: 你在这东张西望干什么？
t_en: What are you doing here? And looking around like this?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}啊…我正在物色助手。
t_en: Ah, it's you, {NICKNAME}... I'm looking for an assistant.

t_ch: 得找熟悉当地情况的、热心的、接受过良好教育、敢于冒险的化外民充当向导，协助我完成在仙舟上的考察项目。
t_en: Someone familiar with the local situation, friendly, well-educated... A capable adventurer... An outworlder... All these qualities must be present to help me finish my research project on Xianzhou.

t_ch: 乖乖，要这么多助手啊？
t_en: Woah, you need that many assistants?

t_ch: 多吗？我不觉得啊。等等…你误会了。我只打算雇一个助手，但因为学术研究的关系，我对这位的要求有些严格。
t_en: That many? I don't think so. Wait, I think you misunderstood. I just want to hire ONE assistant, but as this is for my academic research, I have slightly strict requirements for this person.

t_ch: 找助手还是给故事选主角？
t_en: Are you looking for an assistant or the protagonist of a story?

t_ch: 这要求高吗？对于学会的研究来说，这些不过是一个助手的基本要求。
t_en: You think I'm asking for too much? These are just the basic requirements for an assistant when it comes to the Guild's research.

t_ch: 咱们这些博识学会的学士必须深入各个异族文明，赢得当地人的信任；还得冒着天灾人祸，风险可不逊于无名客。
t_en: We scholars from the Intelligentsia Guild must deeply penetrate into alien civilizations and win their trust, while also braving any natural disasters we may encounter. It's as dangerous as being a Nameless.

t_ch: 你瞧瞧，星核一来，天灾人祸。我对仙舟医学史的考察计划又打了水漂。
t_en: See? Now there's a Stellaron causing calamity here. My plans to research the medical history of the Xianzhou just went down the drain.

t_ch: 他们原本批复让我进入丹鼎司参详医典，做些学术交流工作，但事情拖到了现在。仙舟人等得起，老头子我可等不起。
t_en: The locals originally gave approval for me to enter the Alchemy Commission, browse medical texts, and exchange research findings with others, but they kept delaying my visit. Xianzhou residents might be able to afford the wait, but my old bones simply can't.

t_ch: 鄙人陶德•雷奥登绝不拖欠薪资，还会给{NICKNAME}缴纳安全险和双倍加班补贴。
t_en: I, Todd Riordan, hereby humbly promise that I will never withhold pay, and will provide {NICKNAME} with personal insurance and double pay for any overtime.

t_ch: 怎么样，这誓言够神圣吧？
t_en: How is that oath? Good enough?

t_ch: 丹鼎司里出了不少大事…
t_en: A lot has happened in the Alchemy Commission...

t_ch: …你怎么知道的？
t_en: ... How do you know that?

t_ch: 云骑军已经入驻戒严了。
t_en: The Cloud Knights are already imposing a curfew over there.

t_ch: 什么？那可大大不妙了！
t_en: What? That won't do!

t_ch: 等等，绕了一大圈，你好像很了解丹鼎司的内部情况嘛！我听说罗浮将军破天荒地邀请了几位化外民参赞内务…难不成就是你？
t_en: Wait... I've been looking everywhere, but you seem to know a lot about what's happening inside the Alchemy Commission! I heard that the general of the Luofu invited some outworlders to get involved in their internal affairs in a rare turn of events... Could you be one of those people?

t_ch: 真是走运！{NICKNAME}，有没有兴趣做我的助手……
t_en: What luck! {NICKNAME}, are you interested in becoming my assistant...?

t_ch: 想一想，你会作为陶德•雷奥登的合作伙伴被载入学会的教材，被银河中每个历史学讲师频繁提起。这样的条件对你有没有吸引力？
t_en: Just think about it. You will be listed in the Guild's textbooks as Todd Riordan's collaborator, and frequently mentioned by every history lecturer in the cosmos. Does this sound attractive to you?

t_ch: 孩子，这些长生种都是一伙儿的。
t_en: My child, all these long-life species are the same.

t_ch: 我恐怕满足不了你的要求。
t_en: I don't meet your requirements, though.

t_ch: 忘了那些狗屁要求吧。我用我倍加珍惜的学术声望起誓：
t_en: Forget about those darn requirements! I swear with my invaluable academic reputation—

t_ch: 您另请高明吧。
t_en: Please go find someone else.

t_ch: 先别急着拒绝，我能开出的报酬可是非常优厚的。现在仙舟上大乱方平，许多化外民的生计都颇为艰难……
t_en: Don't be in a hurry to reject me! I can offer you a hefty pay. The Xianzhou is in utter chaos right now, and many outworlders are struggling to even earn enough money to eat...

t_ch: 什么，对钱没有兴趣？我懂，钱无法让人获得真正的快乐。我最快乐的时候也是站在讲台上传道授业的时候。
t_en: What? Not interested in money? I understand. Money doesn't bring true happiness. I, too, am the happiest when standing behind a lectern and teaching students.

t_ch: 我能回报你的不止是钱，还有名声和知识。
t_en: I can give you more than just money! I can give you prestige and knowledge!

t_ch: 可我还有很多事要忙。
t_en: But I've got other things to do.

t_ch: 我明白，我明白。所以咱们就从最简单的开始，只需要一次跑腿，去一趟丹鼎司，替我捎点东西回来，可简单了。
t_en: I understand, I understand. We can start with the most simple task. I just need you run one errand for me. Just go to the Alchemy Commission and pick something up. It's super simple.

t_ch: 具体要帮你做些什么呢？
t_en: What exactly do I need to do?

t_ch: 放心，咱们先从简单的跑腿开始。
t_en: Don't worry. We'll start with running an errand, the most simple task of all.

t_ch: 我想拜托你去丹鼎司里取些研究资料。不会耽误你很久的，看在大家都是化外民的面子上，帮个忙吧，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}！
t_en: I want to ask you to go to the Alchemy Commission to pick up some research materials. It won't take too long. Please help a fellow outworlder, {F#young lady}{M#young man}!

t_ch: 说起来，我想先要一本名叫《要药分剂》的仙舟医典。能不能请你跑一趟丹鼎司，帮我把它带来？
t_en: Say, I need a Xianzhou medical textbook called the Compendium of Remedies. Could you please head over to the Alchemy Commission and bring it to me?

t_ch: 我说过，对方同意了我的借阅请求。
t_en: I've stated that they've already approved my request to borrow it.

t_ch: 去吧，我把批复的手续给你，别让我老人家等太久了。毕竟上了年纪，关节炎每一分钟都在惩罚我。
t_en: Go. I'll give you the approval documents. Don't let an old man like me wait too long. At my age, arthritis is a veritable torment.

t_ch: 医典，我的医典来了吗？
t_en: My medical textbook! Is my textbook here?

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}，老家伙我的关节就要断了，你行行好吧。
t_en: Ahh, {NICKNAME}! My old joints are about to break! Please, have mercy on me!

t_ch: 不告而取，你要我去偷？
t_en: There was no approval. Did you want me to STEAL the book?

t_ch: 读书人的事情…我们可不可以用词文雅些？换个词，比如「借阅」。
t_en: Look, we are both educated people... Could we use a less accusatory word? Perhaps "borrow"?

t_ch: 医典应该是丹鼎司机密吧？
t_en: These medical textbooks are the Alchemy Commission's secrets, no?

t_ch: 这算哪门子机密啊。这些医典对丹士而言只是随手可以翻阅的辞书而已。何况，他们也同意我借阅了。
t_en: How are these books secrets? They are just references that alchemists can browse at any time. Besides, they agreed to let me borrow it.

t_ch: 在会见丹鼎司的丹士长时，她批复了我的请求。
t_en: The Alchemy Commission's chief alchemist approved my request when I met her.

t_ch: 按照学会的宗旨，知识需以同样珍贵的知识交换，我给了她一份古老文献的影本。而她回赠了我一纸学会从未收录过的丹方。
t_en: According to the Guild's creed, knowledge should be exchanged with an equally valuable piece of knowledge. I gave her a copy of an ancient text, and she gifted me a prescription the Guild had never seen.

t_ch: 我连药方都没开完就被「请」了出来。
t_en: I got "asked" to leave before I even finished writing the prescription.

t_ch: 没办法，现在到处都乱糟糟的——
t_en: It can't be helped. It's a mess everywhere...

t_ch: 呃，你好，你有什么事吗？
t_en: Um, hello? How can I help you?

t_ch: （向他们借阅医典）
t_en: (Ask to borrow the textbook from them)

t_ch: 《要药分剂》？我记得我前天刚拿来查过某个方子……
t_en: Compendium of Remedies? I remember looking up a prescription in it two days ago...

t_ch: 看你的打扮，是来瞧病的化外民么？你找这个干什么？难不成想自己开方子？
t_en: Your clothes... Are you an outworlder here for treatment? Why do you want that book? Are you trying to write prescriptions for yourself?

t_ch: 胡闹！到了这儿自会有医士为你看诊的，你想自个照着医书胡来可不行。
t_en: How preposterous! Since you're already here, a healer will attend to you sooner or later. You can't treat yourself with a medical textbook.

t_ch: （转述陶德的请求）
t_en: (Recount Todd's request)

t_ch: 博识学会的借阅申请啊？我好像听过类似的事。
t_en: The Intelligentsia Guild's application to borrow textbooks? I think I've heard about this.

t_ch: 唉，司部经此大乱，同僚们都挨个接受云骑审查去了。眼下你的请求怕是谁都一问三不知。
t_en: *sigh* With the commission in chaos, all of our colleagues are being interrogated by the Cloud Knights. I don't think anyone would know about the request you're talking about.

t_ch: 不过新上任的丹士长玉络大人也许能给你答复。
t_en: However, the new chief alchemist, Lady Yuluo, might be able to help you.

t_ch: 我记得她就在附近，你可以去问问她。医典外借这种事，向丹士长大人咨询是再合适不过了。
t_en: I think she's somewhere around here. You can try asking her. It's best to ask the chief alchemist about important businesses such as outsiders borrowing our medical texts.

t_ch: （转述陶德的请求）
t_en: (Recount Todd's request)

t_ch: 《要药分剂》？博识学会的申请？
t_en: Compendium of Remedies? The Intelligentsia Guild's application?

t_ch: 我明白了，那是丹枢大人在位时批准的请求…唉，我又忘了，现在不该称她为大人了。
t_en: I see. It was something Lady Dan Shu approved when she still held the post... Oh, I'm sorry. I shouldn't call her "Lady" anymore.

t_ch: 我不想因人废政。但她犯的事，你想必听说了吧？云骑正在严查司内，连正常的医务工作也受到了干扰，真是的……
t_en: I don't want to change her policies, but you must've heard about her crimes, right? The Cloud Knights are thoroughly investigating the commission, and even our day-to-day job as healers are being affected...

t_ch: 唉，虽然我认为丹枢的问题不该牵连到司内正常的学术工作。
t_en: *sigh* I don't think Dan Shu's crimes should influence the academic work being carried out in the commission.

t_ch: 但规矩就是规矩，丹鼎司大乱后，所有的手续都要被复核了。
t_en: But rules are rules. After what happened in the Alchemy Commission, everything has to be re-approved.

t_ch: 一棵普通的树，风穿过树梢的时候，不知为何传来「哗啦哗啦」的纸页声。
t_en: This is an ordinary tree. You can somehow hear the sound of flipping pages as wind blows through the leaves.

t_ch: 你顺着声音的源头仔细寻找，发现一册书不知为何悬挂在树枝上，有一种凌乱的美感。
t_en: You carefully search the tree for the source of the sound and find a book hanging on the branches. The scene gives off a crude sense of beauty.

t_ch: 难道这就是玉络口中的医典？你决定——
t_en: Could this be the textbook that Yuluo mentioned? You decide to...

t_ch: 深吸一口气，抬腿踹向树。
t_en: Take a deep breath and kick the tree.

t_ch: 你深深地吸了一口气，将力量往下凝集在腿部，踹向树身。
t_en: You take a deep breath and put power into your legs, then kick the tree.

t_ch: 很遗憾，这棵树不为所动，回应你的只有凝固的寂静，那册书与你之间凭空生出了一种疏离感。
t_en: Unfortunately, the tree doesn't even shake. Only stupefied silence answers you. The book now suddenly feel more distant than ever.

t_ch: 跳起来触碰枝条。
t_en: Jump up and reach for the branch.

t_ch: 你用力跳了起来，举高双手试图触碰树枝，在起跳的瞬间，时间凝滞了。
t_en: You jump as high as you can and lift your arms to try to reach the branch. Time stops the moment your feet leave the ground.

t_ch: 你疑心自己是否会摸到了什么不该摸到的东西，比如一个鸟巢或是蜂窝。
t_en: You are concerned that you might reach something you shouldn't touch, such as a bird nest or a beehive.

t_ch: 在你若有所思的同时，一册书从树上掉落。
t_en: As you ponder about this, a book falls from the tree.

t_ch: 趁眼下没人，拿武器用力砸树。
t_en: Hit the tree with your weapon! No one is watching.

t_ch: 你掏出了你最趁手的宝贝，铆足了力气挥舞。
t_en: You take out your favorite treasure and swing it as hard as you can.

t_ch: 树身上传来的反作用力令你差点摔倒在地。你这才意识到仙舟上的树似乎不是普通的树，至少这一棵不是。
t_en: The rebound from the tree almost knocks you over. Only now do you realize the Xianzhou's trees don't seem to be ordinary trees... Not this tree, at least.

t_ch: 你早就怀疑过地衡司在人工植被上的审美，又或者这是生物工程特殊栽培的产物——树身竟然是某种坚硬的金属！
t_en: You've long had doubts about the Realm-Keeping Commission's aesthetics when it comes to artificial greenery. Alternatively, maybe this is a product of biological engineering — The tree trunk is made of some kind of hard metal!

t_ch: 陶德就地翻阅了起来…
t_en: Todd starts reading on the spot...

t_ch: 这张药方虽然在医典记载中存在，却多加了一味来历不明的药材？
t_en: This prescription is recorded in the medical textbooks, but there's an extra ingredient.

t_ch: 方子上并没有细说它的来源和性质，只是称它为「天人金枝」？仙舟的常用药材中有叫这么个名字的吗？
t_en: The prescription didn't say what this ingredient is or where it's from. It's just called "Divine Aureate Twig." Is this the name of a common medicine in Xianzhou?

t_ch: 医典借来了。
t_en: I got the textbook.

t_ch: 是嘛？让我瞧瞧。
t_en: Really? Let me see.

t_ch: 唔，从书上看来，她给的药方确有其物，但其中的药材又是怎么回事？
t_en: Hmm, it looks like the prescription she gave does exist in the book, but what about the ingredients in it?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你有什么眉目吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you think?

t_ch: 谜题的答案总会指向另一个谜。
t_en: The answer of one question will always lead to another.

t_ch: 你在研究什么？
t_en: What are you researching?

t_ch: 我想搞清楚之前那位丹士长赠给我的药方究竟是真是假。
t_en: I want to find out whether the prescription the last chief alchemist gave me is genuine.

t_ch: 就这样了？
t_en: That's all?

t_ch: 当然不会，这只是我研究的开始罢了。而我首先要解决一个心头疑问：那位丹士长赠给我的药方究竟是真是假。
t_en: Of course not. This is only the start of my research, but the question I want to answer the most is whether the prescription the last chief alchemist gave me is genuine.

t_ch: 毕竟我可是送出了《冥土还魂论》这般珍贵的古籍影本，当然得验验她这份礼物的成色。
t_en: After all, I gave her a copy of Theories on Returning From the Underworld, which is an invaluable ancient text. Of course I need to see how good her returning gift is.

t_ch: 那个女人的礼物一定包藏祸心…
t_en: That woman's gifts always contain evil intent...

t_ch: 怎么，你和她打过交道？
t_en: Oh? You've dealt with her?

t_ch: 放心吧，她精通药理。
t_en: Don't worry. She is skilled in pharmacology.

t_ch: 喔？你找她看过病？
t_en: Oh? She's treated you before?

t_ch: 按这个抓药服一下不就知道了。
t_en: Just make this medicine and ingest it to find out.

t_ch: 短生种服长生种的猛药？老头子我还想活久一些呢。
t_en: You want a short-life species to take a long-life species' powerful medicine? I want to live for a few more years still!

t_ch: 我猜，是魔阴身的枝叶？
t_en: I'm guessing it's a twig from the bodies of the mara-struck.

t_ch: 我也不知道…
t_en: I don't know either...

t_ch: 魔阴身？为什么仙舟人要用这么古怪的东西入药？
t_en: The mara-struck? Why would Xianzhou natives use such odd stuff in their medicines?

t_ch: 陶德疑惑地看向你。
t_en: Todd looks at you, puzzled.

t_ch: 哼，但你说的很有道理，我也见过那些浑身长出枝叶的妖怪。看来咱们的第二个任务出现了。
t_en: Hmm, but it does make sense. I've seen these monsters with branches on their bodies. Looks like we've got another mission.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，把这玩意儿的样本带一份来吧。这对你来说不难吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, bring me a sample of this ingredient. It shouldn't be too hard for you, right?

t_ch: 你、你好？
t_en: H—Hello?

t_ch: （询问陶德所要的药材一事）
t_en: (Ask about the ingredient Todd wants)

t_ch: 呃，抱歉，我其实才被选入丹鼎司不久，方子我还没背熟呢。
t_en: Um, sorry, I just started working in the Alchemy Commission recently. I haven't even memorized the common prescriptions yet.

t_ch: 你问的这个我实在不知道，你还是去问其他人吧。
t_en: I really can't answer this question. It's better if you ask other people.

t_ch: 有什么事就直接说。
t_en: Just spit it out already.

t_ch: （询问陶德所要的药材一事）
t_en: (Ask about the ingredient Todd wants)

t_ch: 你问丹方的问题？那就去问丹士啊，我学的是手术，开膛破肚的那种，怕了吧？
t_en: You want to know about prescriptions? Then go ask an alchemist. My specialty is in surgery. The kind that opens people up. Scared yet?

t_ch: ……好家伙，面不改色啊。那就换你能听懂的：找个懂行的问吧，我、我专业不对口。
t_en: ... Wow, aren't you a tough one. Fine, I'll say it in another way: Go find a real professional. This... This isn't my expertise.

t_ch: 化外民？有什么事吗？如果寻医问药，去行医市集挂号求诊吧。
t_en: An outworlder? How can I help you? If you are after treatment, please go to the Healer's Market and make a booking.

t_ch: （询问何为「金枝」）
t_en: (Ask about the "Aureate Twig.")

t_ch: 丹士的表情突然紧张了起来。
t_en: The healer suddenly looks very nervous.

t_ch: 你是从哪儿得知这个名字的？
t_en: Where did you hear that name?

t_ch: 化外民能翻阅的医典里绝不会写上这等禁忌之物的名称。
t_en: The medical texts that outworlders have access to would never contain the name of such a forbidden entity.

t_ch: 你是「药王秘传」的残党？
t_en: Are you a remnant of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus?

t_ch: 哼，你如果不是药王莳者，便是曾见过魁首大人，受赐仙方。
t_en: Hmph! If you're not a Sanctus Medicus disciple, then you must have met the master primus and received her prescriptions.

t_ch: （告知陶德所要的药材一事）
t_en: (Recount about the ingredient Todd wants)

t_ch: 我拿到你要的东西了。
t_en: I got what you want.

t_ch: 辛苦你了。
t_en: Thank you for your hard work.

t_ch: 有意思，这玩意儿确实形如某种植物枝条，但上面的脉络散发金光…其中的秘密一定和仙舟人过去的神话有关。
t_en: Fascinating. This thing does look similar to twigs, but its veins shine with a golden light... Its mysteries must be related to the Xianzhou natives' legends.

t_ch: 贝尼尼学士若是知道了我的收获，一定会嫉妒的。
t_en: Scholar Bernini will be so jealous once he finds out about what I've learned here.

t_ch: 陶德将诡异的枝条制成样本。
t_en: Todd makes the eerie twig into a specimen.

t_ch: 虽然手头的工具不多，但学会耽搁许久的研究也算有了些进展了。谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I don't have a lot of tools available to me here, but the Guild's research finally has some progress after a long delay. Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 说的不错，该是兑现誓言的时候了。
t_en: Well said, and now it's time for me to fulfill my promise.

t_ch: 你帮了我这么多忙，我也不是个什么小气的人。除了看得见摸得着的赏金外，我还提供博识学会最常见的报酬，知识——知识远胜黄金。
t_en: You've helped me so much, and I'm definitely not a stingy person. Apart from tangible cash, I will also provide the most common reward in the Intelligentsia Guild — that of knowledge. After all, knowledge is worth more than gold.

t_ch: 这次行动我所整理的知识将会同你共享，拿好这份手稿吧。如果有进一步考察的需要，我还会找你的，孩子。
t_en: I will share with you all the information I gain from this project. Take this manuscript and keep it safe. I'll come find you again, young one, if I need to conduct more research.

t_ch: 助手可不好找啊…
t_en: It's difficult to find an assistant...

t_ch: 怎么不找仙舟当地人？
t_en: Why don't you want Xianzhou locals?

t_ch: 我到仙舟一年了，成天被带着游逛景点，灌些难喝的饮料。他们不打算和我分享过去的秘密，就连去丹鼎司的申请都是我再三疏通才得到批复的。
t_en: I've been in the Xianzhou for a year already. They keep taking me to tourists spots and make me drink terrible beverages. They don't plan to share the secrets of their past with me. Even the application to the Alchemy Commission was only granted after painstaking networking.

t_ch: 但你就不一样了，你是被将军选中的人，来去方便，不是一般人可比的。
t_en: But you are different. You are chosen by the general and can go into many secret places, unlike normal visitors.

t_ch: 奇怪，这里也对不上……
t_en: Weird. It doesn't match up here either...

t_ch: 呃，你好…请问你有什么事吗？
t_en: Umm, hi... how can I help you?

t_ch: 那就是没办法了？
t_en: So it's not going to work?
